---

## Version 2026.3

This release adds a fully-featured React profile page, React-based team management,
upgrades to Vite 8, switches to the modern `psycopg` driver, and includes several frontend and backend improvements.

### Standalone Frontend Improvements

The [standalone frontend](/front-end/#standalone-front-end) continues to get closer to feature parity with the Django-rendered frontend.

#### Profile Page

The profile page has been completely rebuilt with full account management capabilities:

- **Added email address management** — add, remove, verify, and set primary email addresses directly from the profile page.
- **Added connected accounts management** — connect and disconnect social auth providers from the profile.
- **Added two-factor authentication setup** — enable/disable TOTP-based 2FA, scan QR codes, and manage recovery codes, all from the React frontend.
- **Added a reauthentication page** for confirming access before sensitive operations.
- Improved the profile page layout with card-based sections for a cleaner UI.

#### Teams Management

Teams can now be fully managed from the React frontend:

- **Added a Team Settings page** to the dashboard with team name editing, member list, invitation management, and a danger zone for deleting teams.
- **Added a Team Switcher component** to the dashboard sidebar for switching between teams.
- **Added a `TeamProvider` context** that makes the active team available throughout the React app.
- **Added the ability to resend team invitations** via a new API endpoint.

#### Other Frontend Changes

- **Added a shared `i18n.js` utility** for translation functions (`gettext`, `interpolate`) that works in both Django and standalone frontend contexts.
- **Improved auth page forms** to use proper `<form>` elements with `onSubmit` handlers instead of button click handlers,
  improving keyboard accessibility (Enter key to submit).
- Updated various auth pages (social auth callback, provider signup, email verification, MFA) to use
  consistent shadcn/ui components (`Button`, `Input`, `Label`) instead of DaisyUI class-based styling.
- Added CSS theme tokens for `success` and `warning` colors.

### Vite 8

- **Upgraded Vite from version 6 to version 8.** This includes faster production build times thanks to Rolldown.
  See the [Vite 8 migration guide](https://vite.dev/guide/migration) for details.
- **Upgraded most JavaScript packages to their latest versions**, including React 19.2, React Router 7.13, DaisyUI 5.5, Tailwind CSS 4.2, TypeScript 5.9, and ESLint 10.
- **Migrated from individual `@radix-ui/*` packages to the unified `radix-ui` package.**

### Other Changes

- **Switched from `psycopg2` to `psycopg` (psycopg 3) in development and `psycopg[c]` in production.** Thanks Brennon for the suggestion!
  The production Dockerfile now installs `libpq-dev` and `gcc` for compiling the C extension.
- **Fixed `bootstrap_subscriptions` management command for dj-stripe 2.10+ compatibility.**
  The command now syncs the Stripe Account before syncing products, preventing "Account matching query does not exist" errors.
  Thanks Julian for reporting!
- Added `legacy-peer-deps=true` to `.npmrc` files because `@tailwindcss/vite` (4.2.1) only declares peer support for Vite 5/6/7, not Vite 8.
  This will be removed once Tailwind releases a version with `vite@^8` in its peer dependencies.

*March 17, 2026*
